channel.title: Menu Engineering on bizOpsPlaybook — Practical Business Plans for Solo Entrepreneurs
channel.description: Recent content in Menu Engineering on bizOpsPlaybook — Practical Business Plans for Solo Entrepreneurs

item.title: How to Price Bakery Items in 2026: The Per-Item Costing Formula
item.description: Walk into ten bakeries that opened in 2025 and ask the owner how they priced their croissant. Nine will say “ingredients times three” — the rule of thumb that every culinary school teaches and every accountant for a failing bakery wishes their client had never heard. Ingredients-times-three works for a 60% gross-margin restaurant with a labor model built around minimum-wage line cooks. It doesn’t work for a bakery where the croissant takes 14 minutes of skilled labor to produce and the ingredients cost $0.80.
